tasks.name: Register a 12U player for the cheapest weekday evening spring season program
tasks.description: Register a new player for the Spring 2026 season in the 12U division by selecting the cheapest available session that meets all these criteria: (1) practices on a weekday (Monday–Friday), (2) starts at or after 5:00 PM, and (3) is part of the Spring 2026 season. Success is measured by completing the registration form for that specific session.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage by entering the league URL in the browser address bar.
Click the “Programs” item in the main navigation menu at the top of the page.
On the Programs page, locate the season filter and select “Spring 2026 Season” from the list of available seasons.
In the division or age group filter section, select the “12U” option so that only 12U programs are shown.
Open the day/time filter panel, set the day filter to include only Monday through Friday, and set the time filter so that only sessions with a start time of 5:00 PM or later are displayed.
Use the sort dropdown above the session list and choose the “Price: Low to High” option to sort the filtered sessions by cost.
From the sorted list of sessions, click the “Register” button for the first session in the list, which should be the cheapest 12U Spring 2026 weekday evening option.

On the registration form, enter the player’s information (e.g., name: “Jordan Miller”, date of birth: “03/15/2014”, guardian name: “Alex Miller”, phone: “555-123-4567”, email: “alex.miller@example.com”), select the “Pay in full” payment option, and then click the “Complete Registration” or similarly labeled button to finish the signup.

tasks.name: Save the next 3 upcoming games for the 14U Thunder team to My Schedule
tasks.description: Find the 14U Thunder team’s game schedule, identify the next 3 games occurring after May 1, 2026, and add exactly those 3 games to a personal 'My Schedule' or 'Favorites' list. Success is measured by having exactly three Thunder games after 05/01/2026 added.
steps: From the homepage, click the “Schedule” or “Games” option in the main navigation menu.
On the schedule page, open the division filter and select “14U” so only 14U games are shown.
Open the team filter dropdown and choose the team named “Thunder” from the list of 14U teams.
Find any date filter or calendar control and set the start date to “05/01/2026” so that only games on or after May 1, 2026 are displayed.
Use the sort control (if available) to sort the filtered games by date and time in ascending order so the earliest upcoming games appear first.

Starting from the top of the list, identify the first three games listed for the 14U Thunder team and, for each of those three games, click the “Add to My Schedule” or “Favorite” button associated with that game.
If prompted to choose where to save, select the “My Schedule” list or similar personal schedule option rather than any other list.
Navigate to the “My Schedule” or “My Games” section via the site’s user menu or header link so the three selected Thunder games are stored in that personal section.

tasks.name: Sign up for two evening concession stand volunteer shifts in June at Field 2
tasks.description: Use the volunteer sign-up system to register for exactly two concession stand shifts in June that start at or after 5:00 PM at Field 2. Success is measured by having two and only two June evening concession shifts at Field 2 reserved.
steps: From any page, click the “Volunteer” link in the main navigation or footer to go to the volunteer opportunities page.
Locate the month or date filter and set it to show opportunities in “June 2026” only.
In the location or field filter section, select “Field 2” so that only volunteer shifts at Field 2 are displayed.
Use the role/type filter to select “Concession Stand” so that only concession stand shifts are listed.

Apply a time filter or scan the list to find shifts with a listed start time of 5:00 PM or later, and use a sort option (such as “Date & Time: Earliest First”) so the earliest evening shifts appear at the top.
From the filtered and sorted list, click the “Sign Up” button for the first two concession stand shifts that start at or after 5:00 PM in June at Field 2.

On the volunteer sign-up form for each selected shift, enter the required contact information (e.g., name: “Taylor Smith”, phone: “555-987-6543”, email: “taylor.smith@example.com”) and confirm the selected shift time, then click the “Confirm Shift” or similar button.

After submitting the second shift form, use the “My Volunteer Shifts” or “My Commitments” link (if available) in the account or header menu to navigate to the section where both evening Field 2 June concession shifts are listed for you.

tasks.name: Buy three specific items from the league store with price and rating constraints
tasks.description: Purchase exactly three items from the online league store: (1) one youth T-shirt in size Medium priced under $20, (2) one adult hat priced under $25, and (3) one water bottle with a rating of at least 4.0 stars. Finish by choosing Standard Shipping at checkout. Success is measured by having these three different items in the cart with quantity 1 each and Standard Shipping selected.
steps: Navigate to the “Store” or “Shop” section by clicking its link in the main navigation menu.
In the store categories, click on “Apparel” and then use any subcategory filters to show “Youth T-Shirts” or equivalent, then set a maximum price filter of $20 so only youth shirts under $20 appear.
From the filtered youth T-shirt results, click on a product that has a size option, select size “Youth M” or “Medium” from the size dropdown, and click the “Add to Cart” button once.
Return to the main store categories and select the “Hats” or “Caps” category, then set a maximum price filter of $25 so only hats priced under $25 are shown.
From the filtered hat results, click on the first adult-sized hat product shown, confirm that it is listed as adult sizing (not youth), and click the “Add to Cart” button once.

Go back to the store categories and choose “Accessories” (or similar), then apply a product-type filter to show only “Water Bottles” and a rating filter to show items with a customer rating of at least 4.0 stars.
From the water bottle results with rating ≥ 4.0 stars, click on the first product, review any color or style dropdowns, choose any available color, and click “Add to Cart” once.

Click the cart icon in the header to open the cart, then click the “Checkout” button, and on the checkout page select the “Standard Shipping” option from the shipping method choices before proceeding to the next step in the checkout flow.

tasks.name: Register for the July pitching clinic with the most hours under $120
tasks.description: Compare July pitching clinics and register for the clinic that offers the greatest total training hours while costing no more than $120. If two clinics tie in hours under $120, choose the cheaper one. Success is measured by completing registration for the single qualifying clinic that best meets these criteria.
steps: From the homepage, click the “Clinics & Camps” or similar option in the main navigation to go to the clinics listing page.
On the clinics page, open the month filter and select “July 2026” so that only July clinics are displayed.
Use the skill focus or type filter to select “Pitching” so only pitching clinics are shown in the list.
In the price filter area, set the maximum price to $120 so that only pitching clinics costing $120 or less are visible.

From the filtered list, click on the first two pitching clinics to view each clinic’s detail page one at a time, and on each detail page identify the stated total training hours (for example, “Total: 8 hours” vs. “Total: 6 hours”).

Compare the total training hours for the clinics you viewed and decide which clinic has more hours; if both have the same number of hours, select the clinic with the lower listed price as the preferred option.
On the detail page of the chosen clinic, click the “Register” button to open the registration form for that clinic.

Fill out the registration form with the required information (e.g., player name: “Riley Johnson”, date of birth: “07/02/2013”, guardian email: “riley.parent@example.com”), confirm the clinic selection is the chosen July pitching clinic under $120 with the most hours, and click the “Submit Registration” or similar button to complete enrollment.

tasks.name: Reserve a Wednesday evening 12U practice field with lights between 6–8 PM
tasks.description: Reserve a one-hour practice field slot on a Wednesday between 6:00 PM and 8:00 PM for a 12U team, choosing a field that has lights enabled if multiple options are available. Success is measured by placing a reservation on a lit field during a one-hour block within that time window on a Wednesday.
steps: From the main navigation, click the “Fields & Reservations” or “Field Rentals” link to go to the field reservation page.
On the reservation calendar, use the date picker to select an upcoming Wednesday (for example, select the first Wednesday of May 2026).
Locate the division or team-level filter and set it to “12U” so that only slots available to 12U teams are shown.
Use the time-of-day filter or view settings to show available time slots between 6:00 PM and 8:00 PM on that selected Wednesday.
In the field filter or feature filter, check the option for “Lights” or “Lighted Fields Only” so that only fields with lights are displayed.
Review the available one-hour slots in the 6:00–8:00 PM window and click on the earliest available one-hour slot (e.g., 6:00–7:00 PM) that appears for a field with lights.

In the reservation form that opens, enter the team name (e.g., “12U Lightning”), coach name (e.g., “Morgan Lee”), and contact phone (e.g., “555-222-3344”), then confirm the selected date, time, and field with lights.
Click the “Confirm Reservation” or similarly labeled button to finalize the practice field booking for that Wednesday evening slot.

tasks.name: Find a beginner 10U team within 10 miles that practices on Tuesdays or Thursdays
tasks.description: Use the team finder to locate a team for a 10-year-old beginner within 10 miles of ZIP code 12345 that practices on either Tuesdays or Thursdays. Save that team along with the coach’s contact information to a personal team list. Success is measured by having one team that matches all criteria saved with contact details stored.
steps: From the homepage, click the “Teams” or “Find a Team” option in the main navigation menu.
On the team finder page, select the age division filter and choose “10U” so that only 10U teams are listed.
Locate the skill level filter and choose “Beginner” or “Recreational” to limit results to beginner-level teams.
Use the location or distance filter to enter ZIP code “12345” in the ZIP field and set the radius slider or dropdown to “10 miles” so teams within 10 miles are shown.
In the practice days filter section, select only “Tuesday” and “Thursday” so that teams practicing on either Tuesday or Thursday are included.

From the resulting list, review the practice day and time information for each team and click on the team whose practice time starts earliest in the afternoon or evening on a Tuesday or Thursday (for example, choose a team with a 5:00 PM practice over one with a 6:00 PM practice).
On that team’s detail page, locate the coach’s contact information (such as the email and/or phone number) and click the “Save to My Teams” or similar button to add this team to your personal list.
If a note or label field appears when saving, type “Beginner 10U within 10 miles of 12345” into the notes field and click the “Save” or “Add Team” button to store the team with that description.

tasks.name: Save 12U bat rules and the league Code of Conduct to Saved Resources
tasks.description: Locate the official bat rules for the 12U division and the league-wide Code of Conduct policy, and add both as saved resources in a personal resources area. Success is measured by having two saved items: one labeled or representing 12U bat rules and one representing the Code of Conduct.
steps: From any page, click the “Rules & Policies” or “League Rules” link in the main navigation or footer.
On the rules page, find the division-specific rules section and select “12U” from the division list or dropdown.

Within the 12U rules, scroll or use the section navigation to locate the “Equipment” or “Bat Regulations” subsection and click on the link or heading labeled “Bat Rules” or “Bat Regulations” to open its detail view.
On the 12U bat rules detail view, click the “Save to My Resources,” “Bookmark,” or similar button to add the 12U bat rules to your personal saved resources list.
Use the breadcrumb navigation or a “Back to Rules & Policies” link to return to the main rules and policy list.
In the global policies section, click on the document or link titled “Code of Conduct” or “Player & Parent Code of Conduct” to open its full policy page.
On the Code of Conduct page, click the “Save to My Resources,” “Bookmark,” or similarly labeled button to add the Code of Conduct to your saved resources.
Navigate to the “My Resources” or “Saved Items” section from the account or header menu so that both the 12U bat rules and the Code of Conduct are stored there as saved entries.

entities.name: Division

fields.name: code

entities.name: Season
entities.description: League seasons like Spring 2026 used to filter programs.

entities.name: LeagueProgram
entities.description: High-level league programs within a season and division, parent of specific sessions.

entities.name: ProgramSession
entities.description: Specific practice sessions for a program, filtered by day, time, and price, and used for registration.

entities.name: Player
entities.description: Youth players being registered for programs and clinics.

entities.name: GuardianContact
entities.description: Parent/guardian contact information entered on registration and volunteer forms.

entities.name: ProgramRegistration

entities.name: Team
entities.description: League teams used for game schedules and team finder (e.g., '14U Thunder').

entities.name: TeamPractice
entities.description: Practice schedule entries (day/time/location) for each team, used in team finder filters.

entities.name: Field
entities.description: Fields/locations where games, practices, clinics, and volunteer shifts occur.

entities.name: Game
entities.description: Scheduled league games with division, teams, date/time, and field, filterable on the Games Schedule page.

entities.name: SavedGame
entities.description: Games added to the personal My Schedule list.

entities.name: VolunteerShift
entities.description: Volunteer opportunities (e.g., concession stand shifts) filterable by month, field, role, and time.

entities.name: VolunteerSignup

entities.name: ProductCategory
entities.description: Store categories like Apparel, Hats, Accessories used to browse products.

entities.name: Product

fields.name: available_sizes
fields.description: Array of size option values, e.g., ['youth_m', 'youth_l']

entities.description: Store products such as youth T-shirts, adult hats, and water bottles, with price and rating.

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Shopping cart for the single agent, holding items before checkout.

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Line items in the shopping cart, capturing selected options like size and color.

entities.name: Order
entities.description: Completed store checkout orders, including chosen shipping method (e.g., Standard Shipping).

entities.name: OrderItem
entities.description: Items included in a completed order, mirroring cart item selections.

entities.name: Clinic
entities.description: Clinics and camps (e.g., July pitching clinics) used for comparison and registration.

entities.name: ClinicRegistration
entities.description: Registrations for specific clinics, capturing chosen clinic and participant details.

entities.name: FieldReservationSlot

fields.name: division_id
fields.description: Division allowed for this slot, e.g., 12U

entities.description: Bookable one-hour field time slots with division eligibility, used by the reservation calendar.

entities.name: FieldReservation
entities.description: Confirmed bookings of specific field reservation slots by teams (e.g., Wednesday 6–7 PM with lights).

entities.name: SavedTeam
entities.description: Teams saved to the personal My Teams list along with optional notes.

entities.name: RuleDocument

fields.name: title
fields.description: e.g., '12U Bat Rules' or 'Code of Conduct'

fields.name: division_code
fields.description: 'all' for league-wide policies like Code of Conduct

fields.name: article_id
fields.description: Identifier used in URLs, must include 'code_of_conduct'

entities.description: Rule and policy documents such as 12U bat regulations and the league Code of Conduct.

entities.name: SavedResource
entities.description: Rule and policy documents saved/bookmarked to the personal My Resources list.
